text.title: Social Proof Harvest — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every harvest cycle must enforce. These are HARD rules — eagerness for testimonials cannot override them.

rule.statement: Detect MUST be automated: a saved search, alert, or scraper runs at least daily across every channel where the product gets mentioned organically; manual scrolling does not count as detection.
rule.rationale: Solo founders skip manual detection in busy weeks and lose months of signal; automation removes the human discipline cost.

rule.statement: Capture MUST record: source_url, author_handle, author_display_name, verbatim_quote, captured_at, channel; quotes paraphrased by the founder are rejected at capture time.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased quotes attributed to a real person create legal exposure and erode trust on inspection; verbatim is the only defensible form.

rule.statement: Consent MUST be explicit and logged: a permission request is sent to the author with the exact quote and the exact publication context; the reply ("yes, you may publish") is stored with timestamp before status flips to `approved`.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 6/7 consent requirements + general personality-rights doctrine

rule.rationale: Public visibility on a personal handle is not consent to commercial use; storing the reply is the only defense against a takedown demand.

rule.statement: Publish MUST cite the source: every wall entry links back to the original public mention (tweet URL, post URL, comment URL); broken or deleted source URLs trigger a quarterly "stale-source" review.
rule.rationale: Visitors who can click through and verify trust the quote 4–6x more than uncited blurbs; broken links erode the same trust.

rule.statement: The quote DB MUST be the single source-of-truth: landing page, sales deck, email signatures, and outbound ALL pull from the same record id; duplicating quotes into multiple stores is prohibited.
rule.rationale: Diverging copies create stale or conflicting wording and turn one consent record into many ambiguous claims.

example.title: Anchored harvest entry
example.code: quote_id: q_2026_0142 source_url: https://twitter.com/devjane/status/1234567890 author_handle: "@devjane" author_display_name: "Jane R." verbatim_quote: "Cut my deploy time from 12 minutes to 90 seconds with @ourproduct." captured_at: 2026-05-18T09:14:00Z channel: twitter consent: requested_at: 2026-05-18T10:02:00Z granted_at: 2026-05-18T16:41:00Z granted_via: dm status: approved publish: surfaces: [wall, landing-hero-rotation]
example.note: Verbatim, sourced, consented with timestamp, single record drives multiple surfaces.

example.title: Un-anchored capture
example.code: Someone on Twitter said our deploys are super fast. Pasted into the landing page.
example.note: No source URL, no author identity, no consent, no record id. Unusable and exposed.
